title: WATER-METER-FOUNDATION
scope: USER-DIRECTED water domain, phase 1 of 12 (86-97). Brownfield: scout confirmed water bills today via WaterRateService (3-tier rate building->PaymentConfiguration->config 150) + WaterReadingObserver (consumption=current-previous, cost=consumption*rate, decimal:2) + InvoiceService::generateInvoiceForLease (consumption sums approved+uninvoiced reading.cost; flat_rate returns building.water_flat_rate). Config = payment_configurations(water_billing_type consumption|flat_rate|none, water_unit_rate, flat_water_rate) + buildings(water_billing_type, water_unit_rate, water_flat_rate nullable=inherit). WaterModuleAccess+EnsureWaterModule gate. WaterReading is keyed by unit_id (NOT a meter); units.meter_number is an unused informational string; NO meter/connection table exists. Bulk multi-unit entry, required photo, OCR, below-previous+duplicate validation ALREADY EXIST (do NOT rebuild). RETIRED WaterSetting orphan must not be reused. THIS PHASE = the metering foundation everything rides on: (1) fix the caretaker role-split (caretaker water hub MUST NOT show Settings), (2) introduce a first-class Meter entity with lifecycle (serial/type/status, utility_type-extensible, main<->sub-meter link, replacement-with-continuity, decommission), re-key readings to meters with a backfill, (3) non-zero baseline reading at meter install, (4) spike/implausible-reading flag at entry. SAFETY: do NOT touch the working biller (InvoiceService/WaterRateService) or rebuild existing reading-entry features; meter re-keying must be backward-compatible (one meter per unit = identical billing). WaterConnection (the universal billable entity) is DEFERRED to Phase 87 (tariff/billing rework) per scout to avoid rebuilding the biller twice. billing_mode(apportioned)+water_source config also deferred to 87. Estimated readings deferred to Phase 88 (cadence). Money is decimal:2.

findings.title: Caretaker sees the landlord-only Settings tab in the water hub
findings.evidence: Hub.vue:46 unconditionally pushes the Settings tab to the tab list regardless of canInput/canReview; a caretaker sees it and can open water billing config.
findings.remediation: Add a canSettings prop (landlord-only) and gate the Settings tab + quick-link on it in Hub.vue. Caretaker tab set = overview + readings (+history); NO settings. Phase86 test: caretaker hub payload/render has no settings tab; landlord does.

findings.title: WaterHubController exposes settings data + ?tab=settings to caretakers
findings.evidence: WaterHubController returns getSettingsData() for any tab with no role gate and does not redirect a caretaker off ?tab=settings.
findings.remediation: Add canSettings = isLandlord to the props; only compute/return settings data when canSettings; redirect a caretaker requesting tab=settings to overview. Phase86 test: caretaker GET /water?tab=settings does not receive settings data / redirects.

findings.title: WaterSettingsController allows caretakers to view/update billing config
findings.evidence: WaterSettingsController::index/update authorize isLandlord() || isCaretaker(); water billing configuration is landlord-only.
findings.remediation: Gate WaterSettingsController index + update to landlord-only (abort 403 for caretaker). Keep it ungated by water.module (it is the enable point). Phase86 test: caretaker GET/PUT /water/settings -> 403; landlord OK.

findings.title: Audit caretaker water hub for other landlord-only leaks
findings.evidence: Beyond Settings, the overview stats / quick-links may surface landlord-only financial data (rates, revenue) to caretakers.
findings.remediation: Audit WaterHubController overviewStats + Hub quick-links; ensure caretaker overview shows operational data (pending counts, their buildings) NOT billing rates/revenue. Gate any financial fields behind canReview/isLandlord. Phase86 test: caretaker overview payload omits rate/revenue fields.

findings.title: water_meters table (first-class meter entity + lifecycle)
findings.evidence: Readings key off unit_id; units.meter_number is an unused string; no meter entity, status, baseline, sub-meter link or replacement history exists.
findings.remediation: Migration water_meters: id, landlord_id (TenantScope), building_id (FK), unit_id (FK nullable - free-standing lines allowed later), parent_meter_id (self-FK nullable, for main<->sub), serial_number, utility_type (string default 'water' - extensible to electricity/gas, NOT implemented), meter_type (string nullable e.g. analog|digital), status (enum active|inactive|faulty|replaced|decommissioned default active), initial_reading (decimal:2 default 0 - the non-zero baseline), installed_at (date nullable), decommissioned_at (date nullable), replaced_by_meter_id (self-FK nullable), notes (nullable), timestamps, softDeletes. Indexes (landlord_id,status), (unit_id), (parent_meter_id).

findings.title: Meter model + factory + policy + registration
findings.remediation: App\Models\Meter (TenantScope + HasFactory + SoftDeletes; casts incl initial_reading decimal:2, status enum or string, installed_at/decommissioned_at date; relationships unit/building/landlord/parent/subMeters/readings/replacedBy; scopes active; helper latestReadingValue / currentBalance). MeterFactory. MeterPolicy (before super-admin; viewAny landlord|caretaker; view/manage landlord-owns or caretaker-of-landlord; create/replace/decommission landlord only). Register in AuthServiceProvider.

findings.title: Re-key water_readings to meters with a non-destructive backfill
findings.evidence: water_readings.unit_id only; consumption continuity / replacement needs readings keyed by physical meter.
findings.remediation: Migration adds water_readings.meter_id (FK nullable). Data backfill: for each distinct unit that has readings or a non-null units.meter_number, create one active Meter (serial = meter_number or generated, unit_id, building_id from unit, installed) and set its readings' meter_id; set meter.initial_reading from that unit's earliest reading.previous_reading. Keep unit_id (denormalized for tenant resolution). WaterReading::meter() relationship. Backward-compatible: one meter per unit => identical billing.

findings.title: Consumption computed per-meter (not per-unit), baseline-aware
findings.evidence: WaterReadingObserver computes previous_reading from the unit's prior reading; with meters + replacement this must be per-meter and the first read must use the meter baseline, not 0.
findings.remediation: Update the consumption derivation so previous_reading = the latest prior reading FOR THE SAME METER; when none exists, use meter.initial_reading (non-zero baseline). One-meter-per-unit keeps current behavior identical. Phase86 test: first reading on a meter with initial_reading=500 and current=520 yields consumption=20 (not 520).

findings.title: Meter replacement is an explicit event preserving consumption continuity
findings.evidence: A swapped meter often starts non-zero; a naive reading on the new meter misfires below-previous validation or computes huge/negative consumption.
findings.remediation: MeterReplacementService::replace(old, newSerial, oldFinalReading, newInitialReading): in a transaction record the old meter's final reading, mark it status=replaced + decommissioned_at + replaced_by_meter_id, create the new active Meter (initial_reading=newInitialReading) linked to the same unit. Validation becomes swap-aware (the new meter's first read is measured from its own baseline). Phase86 test: replace then read new meter -> consumption from new baseline; old meter inactive.

findings.title: Meter management surface (list/create/replace/decommission) + baseline entry
findings.evidence: No landlord UI to manage meters or set a non-zero install baseline.
findings.remediation: MeterController (landlord-only, owner-scoped) index/store/replace/decommission + StoreMeterRequest/ReplaceMeterRequest; Pages/Water/Meters/*.vue (or a Meters tab on the landlord hub) reachable from the water hub; set initial_reading at create (the non-zero baseline). Routes role:landlord + water.module. nav-audit clean. Phase86 test: routes registered + landlord can create a meter with initial_reading; caretaker cannot.

findings.title: i18n for meter surface (en/sw/ar parity)
findings.evidence: New meter UI strings need localization with parity.
findings.remediation: Add meter.* keys (or extend water.*) in lang/en + lang/sw + lang/ar with parity. Phase86 test: the three namespaces have identical key sets.

findings.title: Flag implausible (spike) readings at entry
findings.evidence: WaterReadingService rejects below-previous + duplicates but accepts an implausibly high reading (data-entry typo / leak) silently.
findings.remediation: At entry, when consumption exceeds a threshold (config multiple of the meter's trailing average, or an absolute cap) flag the reading (e.g. is_anomalous / a warning surfaced to the landlord review) WITHOUT hard-blocking. Surface the flag in the review tab. Phase86 test: a 10x-average reading is stored but flagged; a normal reading is not.

findings.title: Phase 86 surface + behavioral tests
findings.evidence: New schema/model/policy/routes/role-split need a regression guard.
findings.remediation: Phase86WaterMeterFoundationSurfaceTest: water_meters + water_readings.meter_id schema, Meter model/policy registered, meter routes registered, role-split (caretaker no settings tab/data/403 on settings), lang parity. Phase86WaterMeterFoundationTest: backfill correctness, per-meter baseline-aware consumption, replacement continuity, spike flag, meter create landlord-only. Existing water suite (Phase79*, WaterReadingControllerTest) stays green.

findings.title: Runbook + nav reachability
findings.evidence: Meter lifecycle needs operator docs and the new surface must be reachable.
findings.remediation: Extend docs/runbooks/water.md with the meter lifecycle (entity, baseline, replacement, decommission, spike flag, role-split). nav-audit clean (meters surface reachable). Update memory at closeout.
